title: Writing: Vectorizing Actions
learningGoal: Transform simple verbs into compound verbs based on specific requested nuances (completion, force, benefit).
instructions: You will be given a simple sentence. Your task is to rewrite it using a compound (vector) verb to add the requested nuance.
writingPrompt:
Translate and add the requested nuances:
1. He slept. -> Nuance: Finality/Completion. (He fell asleep).
2. I read the book. -> Nuance: Directed inward/Benefit to self. (I read it up).
3. He broke my phone. -> Nuance: Violence/Recklessness. (He smashed it).
4. She did not fall. -> Nuance: Negative sentence. (Careful with the rule!)
promptGoal: Accurately apply 'jaana', 'lena', 'daalna', and the negative-drop rule.
referenceHints: Sentence 3: Usne mera phone tod daala.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: वह सो गया। मैंने किताब पढ़ ली। उसने मेरा फोन तोड़ डाला। वह नहीं गिरी।
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Woh so gaya. Maine kitaab padh li. Usne mera phone tod daala. Woh nahi giri.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: He fell asleep. I read the book. He smashed my phone. She did not fall.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect application of 'gaya' for completion, 'li' for self-benefit, 'daala' for force, and reverting to the simple verb for the negative sentence.

evaluationCriteria.description: Sentence 2 must use 'padh li'. Note the gender agreement: Kitaab is feminine, so it is 'li', not 'liya'.
Sentence 4 MUST use the simple verb 'nahi giri'. If it uses 'nahi gir padi' or 'nahi gir gayi', deduct points.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: वह सो गया। मैंने किताब पढ़ ली। उसने मेरा फोन तोड़ डाला। वह नहीं गिरी।
acceptableAlternates: Woh so gaya. Maine kitaab padh li. Usne mera phone tod daala. Woh nahi giri.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Accepts transliteration. Strict checking on the negative verb and the gender matching for 'padh li'.

criteria.name: Negative Compound Error
criteria.description: If user writes 'Woh nahi gir padi', reduce score significantly. Negative sentences reject compound verbs.

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You are writing with the flair and accuracy of a native speaker.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the core vectors: Sona + Gaya = Fell asleep. Padh + Li = Read it up. Tod + Daala = Smashed. And never use vectors with 'Nahi'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Pay close attention to sentence 2. 'Kitaab' is feminine, so 'Padh liya' becomes 'Padh li'.

hintText: For Sentence 4, ignore the vector entirely. Just say 'She not fell' (Woh nahi giri).
commonMistakes: Writing 'Woh nahi gir gayi' instead of 'Woh nahi giri'.
